metadata.role_title: Senior AI/ML Engineer - Agentic AI Systems (Python, LangGraph, FastAPI)
metadata.match_reason: JD literally matches candidate: Python, LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL, Docker, Kubernetes, Azure; Gartner Leader 2025 GenAI Emerging Market Quadrant.
metadata.notes: 69 open Tredence jobs on Naukri; Pune and Gurgaon roles also available.

research_summary: Tredence is building an agentic analytics platform (root cause analysis, hypothesis testing, question expansion, automated reporting) using LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL, Databricks, Docker, Kubernetes, Azure. Role is Senior AI/ML Engineer focused on multi-agent system design and event-driven pipeline architecture. Company recently won Google Cloud Partner of Year 2026 (Retail) and ISG Databricks Leader 2026. 1K-5K employees. 7 contacts found across Tier 1 (2 senior AI/ML), Tier 3 (2 IC ML engineers), Tier 4 (3 TA/recruiters). Email pattern: firstname.lastinitial@tredence.com.
jd_full_text:
Senior AI/ML Engineer - Agentic AI Systems

Tredence is building an agentic analytics platform capable of root cause analysis, hypothesis testing, question expansion, and automated report generation.

Key Responsibilities:
1. Agentic Analytics Pipelines: Designing automated workflows that can dynamically generate and refine hypotheses to understand the 'why' behind data fluctuations.
2. Event-Driven Execution: Building systems that trigger actions, diagnoses, or alerts automatically in response to specific data anomalies or pipeline events.
3. Automated Root Cause Analysis (RCA): Utilizing multi-agent systems to sift through complex datasets, trace errors or performance drops to their origin, and provide actionable fixes.
4. Hypothesis Testing: Engineering logic that allows AI agents to formulate multiple scenarios, test them against data distributions, and validate assumptions.
5. Question Expansion and Reporting: Expanding basic user inquiries into deeper exploratory questions, automatically analyzing the results, and generating comprehensive human-readable reports.

Expected Qualifications and Tech Stack:
- LLMs and Prompt Engineering: Strong proficiency in working with advanced LLMs
- Engineering and Integration: Hands-on experience with modern tech stacks including Python, LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector databases, and MLOps practices
- Context Engineering
- Stack: Python, LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL, Databricks, Docker, Kubernetes, Azure
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with agentic AI and multi-agent analytics pipelines -- JD is building agentic analytics for root cause analysis, hypothesis testing, automated reporting. Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques first (Agentic AI, LLM Orchestration, LangGraph per JD), Languages 2nd, Vector DBs 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.research_hook: Posted 3 months ago about production agentic AI being about architecture and governance, not viral demos. Cited MIT GenAI Divide research: 95% of enterprises stuck in high-adoption / low-transformation mode. Won internal Outcome Champion Award for measurable business impact.
contacts.subject: agentic analytics architecture question
contacts.content:
Hi Aslam,

Came across your post on the MIT GenAI Divide research a few months back. The point about 95% of enterprises stuck in high-adoption but low-transformation mode resonated. It matches what I keep running into: teams picking up LangGraph but without the governance layer that makes those pipelines reliable past a demo.

For context: I rebuilt a RAG chatbot at Vivansh into a fully agentic LangGraph system with tool-calling over 10+ platform APIs, 2-stage permission-aware retrieval (250+ permissions per query, no caching because permission profiles change too fast), 80% MRR on 1,000+ queries/day for a 500-user EHS platform. The part that took longest was the state machine design, not the LLM integration. That architecture-first gap you described is real.

Tredence's agentic analytics work is something I've been tracking. If your team is hiring on the ML side, would a 15-min call make sense?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Aslam,

Bumping this in case it got buried. Since I sent that note, I put together a short teardown of the LangGraph state machine from the EHS platform build: the partial-access query splitting and per-turn permission revalidation logic. Happy to share if that is useful context.

If now is not the right time, no worries.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Aslam,

Last note from me. If the team is not actively hiring on ML right now, totally understood. Would still appreciate being on your radar when it opens up.

Good luck with the analytics platform build.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Joined Tredence 1 month ago from IBM as Senior AI Engineer. Fresh hire likely building the agentic AI stack from scratch and may be actively shaping the team.
contacts.subject: LangGraph work at Tredence
contacts.content:
Hi Ashwin,

Saw you joined Tredence from IBM last month as Senior AI Engineer. Good timing given what the team is building on the agentic analytics side.

Two years of production AI on my end: rebuilt RAG infrastructure at Silver Touch from 45% to 95% MRR on 50,000+ queries/month after migrating off FAISS to Milvus, then moved to Vivansh where I built a LangGraph/LangChain agentic system with 2-stage retrieval, tool-calling over 10+ APIs, and 500+ concurrent users. Azure AI Engineer Associate certified.

If you're aware of open ML roles on the team, I'd be glad to have 15 minutes to hear what the build actually looks like. Happy to share what I've been working on in return.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Ashwin,

Following up in case this got lost. Since I reached out, I ran a quick benchmark comparing BGE-M3 vs OpenAI text-embedding-3-large on production EHS data. Happy to share the results if that is relevant to anything the Tredence retrieval stack is working on.

If timing is off, no problem.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Ashwin,

Last note from me. If the team is not hiring on ML right now, that is fine. Would appreciate being kept in mind for when it comes up.

Either way, good luck settling into the new role.

Amit

contacts.subject: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production LangGraph/RAG
contacts.content:
Hi Aruna,

Reaching out directly because I think my background fits what Tredence hires for on the AI/ML side.

Short version: 2 years of production AI. LangGraph/LangChain agentic systems running at 500+ concurrent users and 1,000+ queries/day, RAG infrastructure from 45% to 95% MRR on 50,000+ queries/month, multi-model orchestration (GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Qwen3-Coder), FastAPI on Azure. Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate certified. Most recent role is AI Engineer at Vivansh Infotech; before that, AI/ML Developer at Silver Touch Technologies in Ahmedabad.

Saw you post Tredence open roles regularly on LinkedIn. If the Senior AI/ML Engineer - Agentic AI Systems position is still open, I'd appreciate a pointer on next steps.

Resume and live projects: amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

Thanks,
Amit
